_metadata.description: AWS to Azure service mapping for migration assessment. Keys are normalized AWS CloudFormation resource type identifiers.

aws::ec2::instance.description: Virtual machines on-demand. Both support Windows/Linux, custom images, auto-scaling, and spot/low-priority instances.
aws::ec2::instance.migrationNotes: Map EC2 instance types to Azure VM sizes (e.g., m5.xlarge → Standard_D4s_v5). Use Azure Migrate for discovery and assessment.

aws::autoscaling::autoscalinggroup.description: Auto-scaling groups of identical instances/VMs. Both support scaling policies, health checks, and load balancer integration.
aws::autoscaling::autoscalinggroup.migrationNotes: Map Auto Scaling Groups to VMSS with Flexible orchestration. Convert launch templates to VM image definitions.

aws::ec2::volume.description: Managed block storage for instances. Both offer SSD/HDD tiers, snapshots, and encryption.
aws::ec2::volume.migrationNotes: Map EBS types: gp3/gp2 → Premium SSD v2 or Premium SSD, io2 → Ultra Disk, st1 → Standard HDD, sc1 → Standard HDD.

aws::ec2::snapshot.description: Point-in-time disk snapshots stored in object storage.
aws::ec2::snapshot.migrationNotes: Export EBS snapshots, convert to VHD format, upload to Azure as managed disk snapshots.

aws::ec2::ami.description: Custom machine images for repeatable deployments.
aws::ec2::ami.migrationNotes: Export AMIs as VMDK/VHD, upload to Azure Compute Gallery. Rebuild using Azure Image Builder for optimization.

aws::lambda::function.description: Event-driven serverless compute. Both support multiple languages, triggers, and pay-per-execution.
aws::lambda::function.migrationNotes: Map Lambda handlers to Azure Function triggers. Review timeout limits (Lambda 15min vs Functions 10min consumption). Consider Flex Consumption plan.

aws::s3::bucket.description: Object storage service. Both support tiered storage, lifecycle policies, versioning, and static website hosting.
aws::s3::bucket.migrationNotes: Map S3 tiers: Standard → Hot, S3-IA → Cool, Glacier → Archive. Use AzCopy or Azure Data Box for large migrations.

aws::efs::filesystem.description: Managed file storage (NFS/SMB). Both support elastic scaling and multi-client access.
aws::efs::filesystem.migrationNotes: EFS (NFS) maps to Azure Files NFS or Azure NetApp Files for high performance. Map EFS Standard → Premium Files, EFS IA → Standard Files.

aws::fsx::filesystem.description: High-performance managed file storage for enterprise workloads.
aws::fsx::filesystem.migrationNotes: FSx for NetApp ONTAP → Azure NetApp Files. FSx for Windows → Azure Files (SMB). FSx for Lustre → Azure Managed Lustre.

aws::rds::dbinstance.azureService: Azure SQL Database / Azure Database for MySQL/PostgreSQL
aws::rds::dbinstance.description: Managed relational database service. Both support multiple engines, automated backups, and read replicas.
aws::rds::dbinstance.migrationNotes: Map engine: RDS MySQL → Azure Database for MySQL, RDS PostgreSQL → Azure Database for PostgreSQL, RDS SQL Server → Azure SQL Database. Use Azure Database Migration Service.

aws::rds::dbcluster.azureService: Azure SQL Database (Hyperscale) / Azure Cosmos DB for PostgreSQL
aws::rds::dbcluster.description: Multi-node managed database cluster with read replicas and automated failover.
aws::rds::dbcluster.migrationNotes: Aurora MySQL → Azure MySQL Flexible Server (HA). Aurora PostgreSQL → Azure PostgreSQL Flexible Server (HA). Aurora Serverless → Azure SQL Serverless.

aws::dynamodb::table.description: Globally distributed NoSQL database. Both support single-digit ms latency, auto-scaling, and multi-region writes.
aws::dynamodb::table.migrationNotes: Use Cosmos DB Table API for drop-in replacement or NoSQL API for advanced features. Map DynamoDB RCU/WCU to Cosmos DB RU/s.

aws::elasticache::cachecluster.migrationNotes: ElastiCache Redis → Azure Cache for Redis. ElastiCache Memcached → Azure Cache for Redis (compatible mode). Map node types to Azure Cache tiers.

aws::elasticache::replicationgroup.description: Managed Redis cluster with replication and automatic failover.
aws::elasticache::replicationgroup.migrationNotes: Map to Azure Cache for Redis Premium/Enterprise tier with clustering enabled. Use Azure-managed failover.

aws::redshift::cluster.description: Managed data warehouse for analytics at petabyte scale.
aws::redshift::cluster.migrationNotes: Map Redshift node types to Synapse DWU. Use Synapse dedicated SQL pool. Consider Synapse serverless for ad-hoc queries.

aws::ec2::vpc.description: Isolated virtual network with subnets, route tables, and security controls.
aws::ec2::vpc.migrationNotes: Map VPC CIDR blocks to VNet address spaces. Map security groups to NSGs. Map route tables to Azure UDR.

aws::ec2::subnet.description: Network subdivision within a virtual network.
aws::ec2::subnet.migrationNotes: Map public/private subnet patterns to Azure subnet + NSG combinations. Azure subnets are always private; use NAT Gateway for outbound.

aws::ec2::securitygroup.description: Stateful firewall rules for network traffic filtering.
aws::ec2::securitygroup.migrationNotes: Map inbound/outbound rules. Note: Azure NSGs support priority-based ordering and service tags. Security groups per-instance → NSG per-subnet or per-NIC.

aws::elasticloadbalancingv2::loadbalance.description: Layer 4/7 load balancing for distributing traffic across instances.
aws::elasticloadbalancingv2::loadbalance.migrationNotes: ALB → Azure Application Gateway (L7). NLB → Azure Load Balancer (L4). GLB → Azure Gateway Load Balancer. Map target groups to backend pools.

aws::cloudfront::distribution.description: Global content delivery network with edge caching and SSL termination.
aws::cloudfront::distribution.migrationNotes: CloudFront → Azure Front Door (advanced routing + WAF) or Azure CDN (simple caching). Map behaviors to Front Door rules engine.

aws::route53::hostedzone.description: Managed DNS hosting service with global anycast network.
aws::route53::hostedzone.migrationNotes: Export Route 53 zone files, import into Azure DNS. Map alias records to Azure alias record sets. Health checks → Azure Traffic Manager.

aws::ec2::natgateway.description: Managed NAT for outbound internet connectivity from private subnets.
aws::ec2::natgateway.migrationNotes: Direct mapping. Map Elastic IPs to Azure Public IP prefixes. Azure NAT Gateway supports multiple public IPs natively.

aws::ec2::vpngateway.description: Managed VPN connectivity for site-to-site and point-to-site connections.
aws::ec2::vpngateway.migrationNotes: Map VPN tunnel configurations. Azure supports IKEv2, BGP, and active-active. Map AWS VGW to Azure VPN Gateway SKU (VpnGw1-5).

aws::directconnect::connection.description: Dedicated private network connection to cloud provider.
aws::directconnect::connection.migrationNotes: Map Direct Connect to ExpressRoute circuits. Check peering locations. Both support 1/10/100 Gbps. Consider ExpressRoute Global Reach for multi-region.

aws::ecs::cluster.migrationNotes: ECS Fargate → Azure Container Apps (serverless containers). ECS EC2 launch type → AKS with node pools. Map task definitions to container app configurations.

aws::ecs::service.description: Managed container service with load balancing and scaling.
aws::ecs::service.migrationNotes: Map ECS service definitions to Container App revisions. Map service discovery to Container Apps ingress. Map Fargate to consumption workload profile.

aws::ecs::taskdefinition.description: Container configuration including image, CPU, memory, ports, and environment.
aws::ecs::taskdefinition.migrationNotes: Map task definition JSON to Container App template spec. Map containerPort to ingress targetPort. Map secrets to Azure Key Vault references.

aws::eks::cluster.description: Managed Kubernetes service. Both support standard K8s APIs, managed control plane, and node autoscaling.
aws::eks::cluster.migrationNotes: Direct K8s workload portability. Map EKS managed node groups to AKS node pools. Map IAM roles for service accounts to Azure Workload Identity.

aws::ecr::repository.description: Managed container image registry with scanning and geo-replication.
aws::ecr::repository.migrationNotes: Push images to ACR. Use ACR Tasks for build automation. Map ECR lifecycle policies to ACR retention policies.

aws::elasticbeanstalk::environment.description: Managed platform for web applications with auto-scaling and deployment slots.
aws::elasticbeanstalk::environment.migrationNotes: Map EB environments to App Service plans. Map EB deployment policies to deployment slots. Supports .NET, Java, Node.js, Python, PHP, Go.

aws::apigateway::restapi.description: Managed API gateway with throttling, caching, authentication, and developer portal.
aws::apigateway::restapi.migrationNotes: Map API Gateway stages to APIM revisions. Map usage plans to APIM products. Map authorizers to APIM policies. Import OpenAPI specs directly.

aws::apigatewayv2::api.migrationNotes: Map HTTP API routes to APIM operations. Consider Azure Functions HTTP trigger as simpler alternative for basic routing.

aws::cognito::userpool.azureService: Microsoft Entra External ID (Azure AD B2C)
aws::cognito::userpool.description: Customer identity and access management (CIAM) with social login, MFA, and custom flows.
aws::cognito::userpool.migrationNotes: Map Cognito user pools to Entra External ID. Map custom auth flows to custom policies. Export users and re-import (passwords require reset).

aws::cognito::identitypool.description: Federated identity for accessing cloud services from applications.
aws::cognito::identitypool.migrationNotes: Map identity pool roles to Azure RBAC assignments. Use Workload Identity Federation for external identity providers.

aws::iam::role.description: Identity-based access control with role assumptions and policies.
aws::iam::role.migrationNotes: Map IAM roles to Azure RBAC roles + Managed Identities. IAM policies → Azure Policy + RBAC role definitions. Cross-account trust → cross-tenant access.

aws::kms::key.description: Managed encryption key management with HSM backing.
aws::kms::key.migrationNotes: Map KMS CMKs to Key Vault keys. Map KMS key policies to Key Vault access policies or RBAC. AWS managed keys → Azure platform-managed keys.

aws::secretsmanager::secret.description: Managed secret storage with automatic rotation support.
aws::secretsmanager::secret.migrationNotes: Migrate secrets to Key Vault. Map rotation schedules. Map resource policies to Key Vault RBAC. Update application references.

aws::wafv2::webacl.description: Web application firewall protecting against OWASP top 10 and custom rules.
aws::wafv2::webacl.migrationNotes: Map WAF rules to Azure WAF custom rules. AWS managed rule groups → Azure managed rulesets. Deploy on Front Door or Application Gateway.

aws::sqs::queue.azureService: Azure Service Bus Queue / Azure Queue Storage
aws::sqs::queue.description: Managed message queuing service with at-least-once delivery.
aws::sqs::queue.migrationNotes: Standard SQS → Azure Queue Storage (simple). FIFO SQS → Azure Service Bus (sessions + ordering). Map DLQ to Service Bus dead-letter.

aws::sns::topic.azureService: Azure Service Bus Topics / Event Grid
aws::sns::topic.description: Pub/sub messaging service with topic-based routing.
aws::sns::topic.migrationNotes: SNS fan-out → Service Bus Topics with subscriptions. SNS event notifications → Azure Event Grid. Map SNS filters to Service Bus subscription rules.

aws::events::rule.description: Event bus for event-driven architectures with routing rules.
aws::events::rule.migrationNotes: Map EventBridge rules to Event Grid subscriptions with filters. Map EventBridge schemas to Event Grid schema registry.

aws::kinesis::stream.description: Real-time data streaming platform for high-throughput event ingestion.
aws::kinesis::stream.migrationNotes: Map Kinesis shards to Event Hubs partitions. Event Hubs has Kafka-compatible endpoint. Map KCL consumers to Event Processor.

aws::stepfunctions::statemachine.description: Workflow orchestration service for coordinating multiple services.
aws::stepfunctions::statemachine.migrationNotes: Step Functions Standard → Logic Apps (visual designer, 400+ connectors). Step Functions Express → Durable Functions (code-first, sub-second). Map ASL to Logic Apps workflow definition.

aws::cloudwatch::alarm.migrationNotes: Map CloudWatch alarms to Azure Monitor metric alerts. Map SNS actions to Action Groups. Map CloudWatch dashboards to Azure Dashboards.

aws::logs::loggroup.description: Centralized log aggregation and analysis service.
aws::logs::loggroup.migrationNotes: Map CloudWatch Logs to Log Analytics workspace. Map Insights queries to KQL. Map log subscriptions to diagnostic settings.

aws::cloudtrail::trail.azureService: Azure Activity Log / Microsoft Defender for Cloud
aws::cloudtrail::trail.description: Audit logging for API calls and resource changes.
aws::cloudtrail::trail.migrationNotes: CloudTrail → Azure Activity Log (control plane) + diagnostic logs (data plane). Map CloudTrail Lake to Log Analytics for long-term analysis.

aws::xray::group.description: Distributed tracing and application performance monitoring.
aws::xray::group.migrationNotes: Map X-Ray traces to Application Insights distributed tracing. Use OpenTelemetry SDK for vendor-neutral instrumentation.

aws::sagemaker::notebookinstance.description: Managed machine learning platform with notebooks, training, and deployment.
aws::sagemaker::notebookinstance.migrationNotes: Map SageMaker notebooks to Azure ML compute instances. Map training jobs to Azure ML pipelines. Map endpoints to managed online endpoints.

aws::sagemaker::endpoint.description: Managed model serving endpoints with auto-scaling.
aws::sagemaker::endpoint.migrationNotes: Map SageMaker endpoints to Azure ML managed endpoints. Map instance types to Azure ML compute SKUs. Export models in ONNX for portability.

aws::bedrock::agent.description: Managed AI agent service with foundation model access.
aws::bedrock::agent.migrationNotes: Map Bedrock models to Azure OpenAI Service models. Map Bedrock agents to Azure AI Foundry agents. Map knowledge bases to Azure AI Search indexes.

aws::iot::thing.description: Managed IoT device connectivity and management platform.
aws::iot::thing.migrationNotes: Map IoT Core things to IoT Hub device identities. Map device shadows to device twins. Map IoT rules to IoT Hub message routing.

aws::iotevents::detectormodel.azureService: Azure Stream Analytics / Azure Digital Twins
aws::iotevents::detectormodel.description: IoT event processing and state machine for complex event detection.
aws::iotevents::detectormodel.migrationNotes: Map IoT Events to Stream Analytics for event processing or Azure Digital Twins for state modeling. More complex migration required.

aws::glue::job.azureService: Azure Data Factory / Azure Synapse Pipelines
aws::glue::job.description: Managed ETL/ELT service for data integration and transformation.
aws::glue::job.migrationNotes: Map Glue ETL jobs to Data Factory data flows. Map Glue crawlers to Data Factory metadata discovery. Map Glue catalog to Azure Purview.

aws::glue::database.description: Data catalog for metadata management and governance.
aws::glue::database.migrationNotes: Map Glue Data Catalog to Purview. Map Glue databases/tables to Purview assets. Map Lake Formation permissions to Purview access policies.

aws::athena::workgroup.description: Serverless SQL query engine for data lake analytics.
aws::athena::workgroup.migrationNotes: Map Athena queries (Presto/Trino SQL) to Synapse serverless T-SQL. Map Athena workgroups to Synapse serverless endpoints. Data stays in storage.

aws::emr::cluster.description: Managed big data processing with Spark, Hadoop, and Hive.
aws::emr::cluster.migrationNotes: EMR Spark → Azure Databricks (preferred) or HDInsight Spark. Map instance fleets to Databricks pools. Map EMR steps to Databricks jobs.

aws::databrew::project.description: Visual data preparation and transformation tool.
aws::databrew::project.migrationNotes: Map DataBrew recipes to Data Factory mapping data flows. Visual transformation logic is similar but syntax differs.

aws::transfer::server.description: Managed SFTP/FTPS service for file transfers to cloud storage.
aws::transfer::server.migrationNotes: Map AWS Transfer Family SFTP to Azure Blob Storage SFTP endpoint. Map user authentication to local users or Entra ID.

aws::cloudformation::stack.description: Infrastructure as Code deployment and stack management.
aws::cloudformation::stack.migrationNotes: Rewrite CloudFormation templates to Bicep. No direct converter exists. Map CFN stack parameters to Bicep parameters. Consider using Terraform as intermediate step.

aws::ssm::parameter.description: Centralized application configuration and feature flag management.
aws::ssm::parameter.migrationNotes: Map SSM Parameter Store to Azure App Configuration. Map SecureString parameters to Key Vault references. Map feature flags to App Configuration feature management.

aws::config::configrule.description: Resource compliance rules and automated remediation.
aws::config::configrule.migrationNotes: Map AWS Config rules to Azure Policy definitions. Map conformance packs to Azure Policy initiatives. Map remediation actions to policy remediation tasks.

aws::organizations::account.migrationNotes: Map AWS Organizations OUs to Azure Management Groups. Map AWS accounts to Azure subscriptions. Map SCPs to Azure Policy at management group scope.

aws::codecommit::repository.migrationNotes: Git clone and push to Azure Repos. Map CodeCommit triggers to Azure Repos webhooks. Consider GitHub as alternative.

aws::codebuild::project.migrationNotes: Map buildspec.yml to azure-pipelines.yml. Map CodeBuild compute types to pipeline agent pools. Map artifacts to Azure Artifacts.

aws::codepipeline::pipeline.migrationNotes: Map pipeline stages to Azure Pipelines stages. Map actions to tasks. Map manual approvals to Azure Pipelines environments with approval gates.

aws::codedeploy::application.azureService: Azure DevOps Release Pipelines / App Service Deployment Slots
aws::codedeploy::application.migrationNotes: Map deployment groups to Azure Pipelines deployment groups. Map blue/green to App Service slots. Map canary to Traffic Manager weighted routing.

aws::appstream::fleet.description: Managed virtual desktop and application streaming.
aws::appstream::fleet.migrationNotes: Map AppStream fleets to AVD host pools. Map AppStream stacks to application groups. Map user settings to FSLogix profiles.

aws::workspaces::workspace.description: Persistent virtual desktop for individual users.
aws::workspaces::workspace.migrationNotes: Map WorkSpaces bundles to AVD VM sizes. Map WorkSpaces directories to Entra ID joined host pools. Use personal host pool type.

aws::backup::backupplan.description: Centralized backup management with policies and retention.
aws::backup::backupplan.migrationNotes: Map backup plans to Azure Backup policies. Map backup vaults to Recovery Services vaults. Map lifecycle rules to retention policies.

aws::guardduty::detector.description: Threat detection and continuous security monitoring.
aws::guardduty::detector.migrationNotes: Map GuardDuty findings to Defender for Cloud alerts. Enable Defender plans per resource type. Map GuardDuty suppression rules to alert suppression.

aws::inspector::assessmenttemplate.migrationNotes: Map Inspector assessments to Defender vulnerability assessment. Enable Defender for Servers for VM scanning. Enable Defender for Containers for container scanning.

aws::macie::session.description: Automated data classification and sensitive data discovery.
aws::macie::session.migrationNotes: Map Macie findings to Purview sensitivity labels. Map custom data identifiers to Purview custom classification rules.

aws::msk::cluster.description: Managed Apache Kafka service for event streaming.
aws::msk::cluster.migrationNotes: Map MSK clusters to Event Hubs Premium with Kafka protocol support. Direct Kafka client compatibility. Map broker configs to Event Hubs settings. Alternatively use Confluent Cloud on Azure.

aws::opensearchservice::domain.migrationNotes: Map OpenSearch indexes to Azure AI Search indexes. Map queries (OpenSearch DSL → OData/simple syntax). Consider Azure AI Search for vector/semantic search capabilities.

aws::neptune::dbcluster.migrationNotes: Map Neptune (Gremlin/SPARQL) to Cosmos DB Gremlin API. Gremlin queries are compatible. SPARQL workloads require refactoring. Map instance types to Cosmos RU/s.

aws::docdb::dbcluster.migrationNotes: Map DocumentDB to Cosmos DB for MongoDB vCore. Wire protocol compatible. Use Azure Database Migration Service for online migration.

aws::memorydb::cluster.migrationNotes: Map MemoryDB to Azure Cache for Redis Enterprise with AOF persistence. Map node types to Enterprise SKUs.
aws::memorydb::cluster.defaultSku: Azure Cache for Redis Enterprise E10

aws::amplify::app.description: Managed hosting for static sites and serverless backends with CI/CD.
aws::amplify::app.migrationNotes: Map Amplify Hosting to Static Web Apps. Map Amplify backend (functions) to SWA API functions. Map custom domains and build settings.

aws::appsync::graphqlapi.migrationNotes: Map AppSync to Azure API Management with GraphQL support or use Hot Chocolate/Apollo Server on Azure App Service. Map resolvers to Azure Functions.

aws::batch::computeenvironment.description: Managed batch computing for large-scale parallel workloads.
aws::batch::computeenvironment.migrationNotes: Map compute environments to Azure Batch pools. Map job definitions to Batch tasks. Map launch templates to pool VM configurations.

aws::mediaconvert::jobtemplate.description: Managed media transcoding and processing service.
aws::mediaconvert::jobtemplate.migrationNotes: Map MediaConvert presets to Media Services transform presets. Map job templates to transforms. Note: Azure Media Services being retired; consider third-party solutions.

aws::ses::emailidentity.description: Managed email sending service for transactional and marketing emails.
aws::ses::emailidentity.migrationNotes: Map SES identities to ACS email domains. Map sending configurations. Map SES templates to ACS email templates.

aws::lightsail::instance.description: Simple, low-cost virtual servers for lightweight workloads.
aws::lightsail::instance.migrationNotes: Map Lightsail plans to B-series VMs. Map Lightsail networking to VNet + NSG. Consider Azure App Service for simple web apps.

aws::apprunner::service.description: Fully managed container service from source code or image with auto-scaling.
aws::apprunner::service.migrationNotes: Map App Runner to Container Apps. Both support source-to-container and image-based deployments. Map auto-scaling configs to KEDA scaling rules.

aws::globalaccelerator::accelerator.description: Global network acceleration and traffic routing.
aws::globalaccelerator::accelerator.migrationNotes: Map Global Accelerator to Azure Front Door (HTTP) or Traffic Manager (DNS-based). Map endpoint groups to backend pools/origins.

aws::datatransfer::bandwidth.description: Azure outbound data transfer pricing. First 100 GB/month free for many services.
aws::datatransfer::bandwidth.migrationNotes: Azure charges for outbound data transfer. Inbound is free. Use CDN/Front Door to optimize.

aws::support::plan.description: Azure Support plans from Basic (free) to Unified (enterprise).
aws::support::plan.migrationNotes: Map AWS Business/Enterprise Support to Azure Standard/Professional Direct/Unified plans.

aws::marketplace::subscription.description: Azure Marketplace for third-party SaaS and VM solutions.
aws::marketplace::subscription.migrationNotes: Check Azure Marketplace for equivalent third-party solutions.
